article.headline: Bayern Munich lose third game in a row as title hopes fade
article.description: Champions Bayern Munich crashed to a 3-2 loss at VfL Bochum on Sunday for a third straight defeat in all competitions that saw them drop eight points behind Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen.
article.linkText: Bayern's title hopes fade with shock Bochum loss

article.story:
Champions Bayern Munich crashed to a 3-2 loss at VfL Bochum on Sunday for a third straight defeat in all competitions that saw them drop eight points behind Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen.

Bayern, who had Dayot Upamecano sent off for the second consecutive match after his dismissal in Wednesday's Champions League loss to Lazio, showed no real game plan with coach Thomas Tuchel now facing mounting pressure.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Bayern had lost last week's big league match against Leverkusen and suffered a 1-0 defeat to Italy's Lazio in their round-of-16 first leg.

"Now [the title] does not look very realistic," Tuchel said. "But last season we believed until the end and we were rewarded. So we will keep working at it."

Referring to the pressure on him following his team's third loss, he added: "That happens after every defeat.

"The 'how' we played is key. The 'how' was good today and that's why today's 90 minutes are very different from the second half at Lazio or the game at Leverkusen."

They were desperate to bounce back and did get a 13th-minute lead when Jamal Musiala drilled in from a tight angle as early pressure paid off.

Bundesliga top scorer Harry Kane should have made it 2-0 but he missed a sitter in front of goal in the 19th, firing wildly over the bar before the game was interrupted.

Fans started throwing tennis balls on to the pitch in protest at a planned foreign investment in the Bundesliga.

Such protests have increased on recent matchdays with almost every league game on Saturday and Sunday having been briefly interrupted.

The 15-minute stop in play did wonders for Bochum's game, with Takuma Asano completing a quick break to stun Bayern with a 38th-minute equaliser.

They completed their comeback before the break when Keven Schlotterbeck headed in a corner in the 44th minute.

Kevin Stoger's 85th-minute penalty put the game to bed after Upamecano was sent off for elbowing an opponent in the box, with a second booking.

Kane tapped in his 25th league goal in the 87th but it did little more than inject some late drama, with Bochum holding on to the three points.

There was also drama after the final whistle with Bayern's Joshua Kimmich leaving for the changing rooms after a heated verbal chat with his team's assistant coach Zsolt Loew.

Bayern, chasing a record-extending 12th straight league title, are in second place on 50 points, eight behind Leverkusen, while Bochum moved up to 11th on 25.

videos.headline: Harry Kane pulls one back for Bayern Munich against Bochum
videos.description: Harry Kane taps in from close range to make it 3-2 as Bayern Munich set up a tense finish against VfL Bochum.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Harry Kane scores to reduce Bochums lead) US Only 20240218

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  VfL Bochum 1848 0, FC Bayern München 1. Jamal Musiala (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Leon Goretzka.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Dayot Upamecano replaces Noussair Mazraoui because of an injury.

Goal!  VfL Bochum 1848 1, FC Bayern München 1. Takuma Asano (VfL Bochum 1848) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Anthony Losilla   following a fast break.
Dayot Upamecano (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Goal!  VfL Bochum 1848 2, FC Bayern München 1. Keven Schlotterbeck (VfL Bochum 1848) header from the centre of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Kevin Stöger with a cross   following a corner.
Bernardo (VfL Bochum 1848) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Anthony Losilla (VfL Bochum 1848) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfL Bochum 1848. Cristian Gamboa replaces Tim Oermann.
Second Half begins VfL Bochum 1848 2, FC Bayern München 1.
Leon Goretzka (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Kim Min-Jae (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leroy Sané replaces Eric Choupo-Moting.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Bryan Zaragoza replaces Joshua Kimmich.
Second yellow card to Dayot Upamecano (FC Bayern München) for a bad foul.
Goal!  VfL Bochum 1848 3, FC Bayern München 1. Kevin Stöger (VfL Bochum 1848) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Mathys Tel replaces Thomas Müller.
Substitution, VfL Bochum 1848. Moritz-Broni Kwarteng replaces Christopher Antwi-Adjei.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Eric Dier replaces Raphaël Guerreiro.
Goal!  VfL Bochum 1848 3, FC Bayern München 2. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Mathys Tel.
Substitution, VfL Bochum 1848. Philipp Förster replaces Takuma Asano.
Substitution, VfL Bochum 1848. Noah Loosli replaces Anthony Losilla.
Second Half ends, VfL Bochum 1848 3, FC Bayern München 2.
